data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Amazon employs a microservices architecture, enabling independent development, deployment, and scaling.
data.technical_spec: Multiple loosely coupled services, each responsible for a specific business function.

data.title: AWS Cloud Infrastructure
data.description: Amazon uses its own AWS infrastructure to host its platform, ensuring resiliency and scalability.
data.technical_spec: Utilization of AWS services like EC2, S3, and RDS for hosting and storage.

data.title: Global Content Delivery Network
data.description: Amazon uses a global CDN to provide fast and reliable content delivery worldwide.
data.technical_spec: Edge locations and distribution strategies to cache content closer to users.

data.title: Event-Driven Architecture
data.description: Events trigger system responses, enabling real-time data processing and high responsiveness.
data.technical_spec: Integration of messaging systems like SNS and SQS for event handling.

data.title: Data Lake Architecture
data.description: A centralized repository stores all structured and unstructured data at scale.
data.technical_spec: Use of services like Amazon Redshift and AWS Lake Formation for data management.
